description: Production Dockerfile — review build artifact security
review_checklist: Check for hardcoded secrets in ENV instructions
Verify base image freshness and pinning
Check for missing USER directive (default = root)

ground_truth.description: Base image ubuntu:18.04 reached EOL April 2023 — no further security patches
AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID hardcoded in image layer — credentials baked into every container instance
AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY hardcoded in image — credential leak via image registry
DATABASE_URL with embedded password hardcoded in image
Installs openssh-server in container — anti-pattern, expands attack surface unnecessarily
ADD downloads installer over HTTPS without checksum verification — supply chain risk
EXPOSE 22 — should not expose SSH on production containers
No USER directive — container runs as root by default. Add 'USER nobody' or non-root UID
